item.title: We Shouldn’t Want a World Run by Prediction Markets
item.description: Prediction markets teach us to look at the future as gamblers, rather than as citizens.

item.title: Dyslexia Is Gavin Newsom’s Superpower
item.description: Donald Trump’s insults alway say more about him than the people he is trying to mock.

item.title: America and Israel United to Fight Iran. Both Will Pay a Price.
item.description: Different tactics and strategies helped the alliance work.

item.title: Get an Electric Vehicle and You Can Stop Worrying About $5 Gas
item.description: We now have cost-competitive alternatives to fossil fuels. What we don’t seem to have is a sense that we’re all in this together.

item.title: Chuck Schumer: What the SAVE Act Would Really Do
item.description: Republicans want to pretend it’s a voter ID bill.

item.title: Mette Frederiksen Changed Denmark
item.description: Whatever Mette Frederiksen does next, Denmark has fundamentally changed.

item.title: The End of the Free-Range ‘Stand by Me’ Childhood
item.description: What a 40-year-old movie told me about childhood today.

item.title: In Odesa, Dressing Up Is Living
item.description: In Odesa, the older generation continues to dress up, showing people’s determination to carry on despite the blows of history.

item.title: Why More U.S. Officials Should Go to China
item.description: Seeing China firsthand can sharpen U.S. policy, reveal Chinese strengths and weaknesses and reduce costly miscalculations.

item.title: Trump Is Hiding the Truth About the War in Iran
item.description: Whatever short-term gain the president thinks he is getting by lying about the war in Iran is far exceeded by the cost, for him, the country and the world.

item.title: Change Your Life. Start a Band.
item.description: We crave connection. We crave community. You should start by recruiting a drummer.

item.title: Bracing for the A.I. Economy to Come
item.description: Readers respond to two guest essays about the changes that artificial intelligence will wreak on the work force. Also: The shoes of the Trump men.

item.title: The Courts Cannot Save Us From Trump
item.description: Placing too much faith in the law mistakes litigation for resistance.

item.title: Let-It-Rip Jeremy vs. Sneaky Sam
item.description: Nothing artificial about this Hollywood A.I. smackdown.

item.title: Trump Has Made a Fundamental Miscalculation about Iran
item.description: America shouldn’t fight a war like this.

item.title: Iran Is a $1.3-Million-a-Minute War
item.description: We could make college accessible for all Americans, restore health care cuts and pretty much end the worst form of global hunger — and still have billions of dollars left over.

item.title: What Is the Left’s Theory of Power?
item.description: The constitutional strength of a functional Congress.

item.title: Trump’s Recklessness Will ‘Haunt Us for a Generation’
item.description: Three Opinion writers on Iran, the SAVE Act and the president’s folly.

item.title: Jay Manuel: What Tyra Banks and I Should Have Known
item.description: For years, Jay Manuel helped create reality television. Now, he finds himself on the other side of the production.

item.title: The Hidden Burdens of Hospice Care
item.description: Readers respond to an essay about a family’s difficult experience providing end-of-life care for a dying relative.

item.title: Trump’s Recklessness Will ‘Haunt Us for a Generation’
item.description: Three Opinion writers on Iran, the SAVE America Act and the president’s folly.

item.title: The Manosphere Continues to Devolve
item.description: A new documentary shows that social media misogyny is just a new way to sell old garbage.

item.title: The Difficulty of Getting Iran’s Nuclear Materials
item.description: Landing in hostile territory and quickly extracting Iran’s highly enriched uranium would be next to impossible.

item.title: The Future of the Democratic Party Is Emerging
item.description: On a new generation of leaders on the left.

item.title: The Last Thing Trump Wants to Do Is Save America
item.description: Trump’s SAVE Act would do anything but save America.

item.title: Naomi Klein on the Fascism of Elite Backlash
item.description: On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the writer Naomi Klein and the Times columnist Ezra Klein discuss how they are thinking about fascism in this political moment — from the elite backlash against movements like #MeToo to the aesthetics and impulsivity of Trumpism.

item.title: ‘The Doppelganger Is at the Wheel’
item.description: Naomi Klein’s book “Doppelganger” is a journey into the world of MAGA and right-wing conspiracies. But it’s also a look at how the left’s political culture sits in relation to this mirror world. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the writer talks to the Times Opinion columnist Ezra Klein about how the use of the “mute button” on social media has spilled offline as well, creating political communities that are isolated from one another.

item.title: Our Hospitals Are Unprepared for the Return of Kids With Measles
item.description: There are lots of follow-on risks once children start getting vaccine-preventable diseases.

item.title: Samuel Alito and Sonia Sotomayor Did Not Attend the Same Princeton
item.description: The university has become more hers than his.

item.title: Trump’s $1.3 Million-a-Minute War
item.description: The war with Iran is costing America. That money, the Opinion columnist Nicholas Kristof argues, could be better spent ending childhood malnutrition, screening for cancer or providing free college tuition.

item.title: Israel’s Lebanese Campaign Will Backfire
item.description: A failure by Israel to engage with the Lebanese government in the fight against Hezbollah will undermine its own interests.

item.title: ‘Once and for All’ Means Never
item.description: Hamas has survived waves of killings of its leaders. More than force will be necessary to overturn the Islamic regime of Iran.

item.title: Joe Kent’s Resignation Letter Is Dangerous Because It’s Half True
item.description: Kent’s resignation letter is partly rooted in truth, even if it taps into old antisemitic tropes about occult Jewish control.

item.title: There’s Only One Path to Victory in Iran
item.description: The U.S. cannot both end the war immediately and claim victory. It is one or the other.

item.title: We’re All Living in the ‘Mirror World’ Now
item.description: Naomi Klein and Ezra Klein discuss what the success of MAGA reveals about the American left.

item.title: He Trolls the Left. What Does He Actually Mean?
item.description: Backlash against Jeremy Carl’s incendiary tweets and language caused him to withdraw his nomination for a State Department post. On “Interesting Times,” Ross Douthat challenges him on his use of phrases like “cultural genocide” and “great replacement.”

item.title: He Thinks Hollywood Has Stigmatized Whiteness
item.description: On “Interesting Times,” Ross Douthat challenges Jeremy Carl on his claim that modern entertainment has stigmatized whiteness.

item.title: White Identity Is Galvanizing the Right
item.description: He wrote a book on anti-white bias. The White House noticed.

item.title: A Shadow Cast Over Cesar Chavez
item.description: Readers react to the Times investigation of the labor leader and civil rights icon. Also: A decision on vaccines; shielding lawyers.

item.title: White Identity Is Galvanizing the Right
item.description: He wrote a book on anti-white bias. The White House noticed.

item.title: Can Military Excellence Save Us From Trump’s Incompetence?
item.description: It should never have come to this.

item.title: Trump Is Putting His Stamp on the World
item.description: Be careful what you wish for.

item.title: Do the Democrats Really Want to Become Populists?
item.description: The Democratic Party is pulling in a populist direction. But not all populisms are equal.

item.title: Trump’s Hypocrisy on Religious Freedom
item.description: Republicans’ attacks on Islam are shameful and full of lies.

item.title: How Trump Should Extricate Himself From His Iran Quagmire
item.description: Declaring victory and ending the war might not be so easy.

item.title: The Alternative to Obama’s Nuclear Deal Was War. Why Did Trump Tear It Up?
item.description: There’s little evidence that the better deal that many opponents demanded was ever within reach.

item.title: Trump’s Ballroom: Put It Underground?
item.description: Readers criticize President Trump’s proposed ballroom addition to the White House. Also: Trust the pediatrician; troops, not “boots on the ground.”
